goal: Explain the practical difference between Rust and the current stack for this project.
deliverable: A project-specific interpretation of what changes if Rust is adopted
constraints: Ground the answer in this repo rather than generic language arguments
Do not implement any code changes in this turn
Separate runtime/language differences from migration and ecosystem differences
acceptance_criteria: The comparison covers development speed, runtime, ecosystem, packaging, database evolution, and validation
The answer identifies what the current stack does better
The answer identifies what Rust would do better later
product_acceptance_criteria: The user can understand what would materially change if the project moved toward Rust
The answer can inform future migration discussion without oversimplifying
manual_spot_checks: Check desktop startup and packaging model
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after the comparison is written, repo-grounded, and clearly bounded as analysis-only output.
delivery_truth_contract: State only confirmed repo facts and explicit engineering judgment; do not imply any migration already exists.
inferred_assumptions: The user wants a practical project-specific comparison.
The biggest concern is what would materially change in delivery and maintenance, not syntax style.
OCR and desktop distribution remain major differentiators for this repo.
